title: What is the difference between the two DVD colections
display_title: What is the difference between the two DVD colections
wikitext:
[]: What is the difference between the two DVD colections?             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Tue Jul 24, 2012 7:12 am What is the difference between the perfect colection and the platinum colection? Not just in what's in them but also picture and sound quality, how accurate the subtitles are and if they contain the original Japanese next episode previews or not. Also do any of them contain region 4 coding?

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Tue Jul 24, 2012 8:00 am Madman in Australia released Platinum for R4, so not sure if that answers your question in that regard... Differences between Perfect and Platinum are on the wiki: http://wiki.evageeks.org/North_American_Video_Releases Consensus is that Platinum is the one to get.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jul 24, 2012 10:59 am There are only two reasons why you would want the Perfect Collection: 1) You're a super nerd (like me) and you want to own all of the things . 2) You want to get Evangelion dubbed in Spanish/French. That's pretty much it. Platinum is better than Perfect in just about every conceivable way. I would recommend getting the Thinpack version, which consists of 6 discs, it strips out all of the frivolous extras and encodes the episodes at a higher data rate. http://www.evamonkey.com/reviews/platinum-thinpak.php

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Jul 24, 2012 12:04 pm 3) You waited until the first DVD release was finished and didn’t anticipate that more enhanced versions of the complete series would come out. Or you are an Eva-01 fan and you like how it is treated in silhouette form with the red eyes on the cardboard box.

[]:             Quote Post by PolarisX » Tue Jul 24, 2012 3:49 pm AR-99 wrote: 3) You waited until the first DVD release was finished and didn’t anticipate that more enhanced versions of the complete series would come out. Or you are an Eva-01 fan and you like how it is treated in silhouette form with the red eyes on the cardboard box.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Wed Jul 25, 2012 2:59 am The Eva Monkey wrote: You're a super nerd (like me) and you want to own all of the things .

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Wed Jul 25, 2012 12:36 pm DID I HEAR SOMEONE SAY AUSTRALIAN RELEASE??? Right, let me list them for you: Firstly, there was the VHS tapes. Then came the original DVD collection, sold both in a box and separately. Along with the original DVD collection came 2 Director's Cuts DVDs with the DC episodes for 21-24. Next was the Platinum release, sold both separately and in a very nice box set form. This set came with all the booklets and extras. Finally the Fatpack of the Platinum collection was released, minus the booklets. Now unfortunately they are all currently out of print.
